frames: The agent starts in a low, crouched posture with all four legs on the ground, appearing stable.
It slightly lifts its front-right leg while maintaining a low body position.
The front-right leg is lowered as the front-left leg is lifted, indicating a slow, alternating leg movement.
The agent continues this alternating pattern, lifting its back-right leg while keeping its body very low.
The back-right leg is placed down, and the back-left leg is lifted, maintaining a low, crawling gait.
The agent lifts its front-right leg again, continuing the slow, deliberate leg movements.
It lowers the front-right leg and lifts the front-left leg, exhibiting a smooth, low-to-the-ground crawl.
The agent completes the leg cycle, placing the front-left leg down and maintaining its low, stable posture.
